top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Mt. Fuji Hill Climb's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
At Mt. Fuji Hill Climb, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Mt. Fuji Hill Climb's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 15 miles.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
307ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
15 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Mt. Fuji Hill Climb. Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Low-cadence force work for 4,600ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Mt. Fuji Hill Climb, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Base fitness for Mt. Fuji Hill Climb. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.
race_overlay.altitude: Mt. Fuji Hill Climb with 4,600ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Over Under to prepare you for 15 miles of mixed terrain in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan.
